title: Disorders of Consciousness Biomarker: Joint Roles of Ignition Capacity and Interoceptive Precision
description: This clinical biomarker study examines whether interoceptive precision (indexed by HEP amplitude, 250–400 ms at Cz) and ignition capacity (indexed by the Perturbational Complexity Index, PCI) jointly predict clinical recovery outcomes across the full disorders of consciousness (DoC) spectrum better than either biomarker alone. Four patient and control groups are studied: VS/UWS (N=30), MCS (N=30), EMCS (N=20), and healthy age/sex-matched controls (N=30); total N=110. Five sub-predictions are tested: (Pred 7.A) joint HEP + PCI model explains more variance in 3-month GCS-S outcome than either biomarker alone (ΔR² ≥ 0.05, AUC > 0.80); (Pred 7.B) HEP amplitude discriminates MCS from VS/UWS; four-group gradient confirmed; DMN↔PCI r > 0.50; DMN↔HEP r < 0.20 (double dissociation); (Pred 7.C) interoceptive perturbation via CCRC (5% CO₂/95% O₂, 90-second) increases PCI ≥ 10% in MCS but not VS/UWS, exceeding arousal-matched white-noise control; (Pred 7.D) HEP amplitude correlates with GCS-S at 3-month and 6-month follow-up (Spearman r > 0.4), with JMbayes2 joint modelling for longitudinal analysis and LOCF explicitly excluded; (Pred 7.E, exploratory) somatic bias modulates reportable embodiment weighting toward 'bodily/visceral' dimensions under high-β attentional focus. Directly supported by Paper 3 Appendix DoC Table.
prediction_cluster_label: Joint Roles of Ignition and Interoception in Disorders of Consciousness (+ Exploratory: Somatic Bias → Embodiment Weighting)

sub_predictions.claim: Joint HEP + PCI model explains significantly more variance in 3-month GCS-S outcome than either biomarker alone.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: ΔR² ≥ 0.05 over best univariate model (primary confirmatory threshold); LRT p < 0.05; AUC > 0.80 (aspirational secondary metric only).
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: Joint model R² ≤ max(univariate HEP R², univariate PCI R²); or ΔR² < 0.05 in primary analysis.

confirming_thresholds.secondary_metric_note: AUC > 0.80 is aspirational secondary metric ONLY — not required for confirmation

falsifying_thresholds.condition_1: Joint model R² ≤ max(univariate HEP R², univariate PCI R²)

sub_predictions.claim: HEP amplitude discriminates MCS from VS/UWS at group level; four-group gradient (VS/UWS < MCS < EMCS < controls) confirmed for both HEP and PCI.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: HEP amplitude MCS > VS/UWS (Mann–Whitney p < 0.05, d > 0.5); four-group ordinal gradient confirmed for both HEP and PCI; DMN–PCI r > 0.5; DMN–HEP partial correlation below DMN–PCI.
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: No significant HEP difference between MCS and VS/UWS; four-group gradient absent for HEP or PCI.

confirming_thresholds.dmn_hep_constraint: DMN–HEP partial correlation must be below DMN–PCI r

falsifying_thresholds.condition_1: No significant HEP difference between MCS and VS/UWS
falsifying_thresholds.condition_2: Four-group gradient absent for HEP or PCI

sub_predictions.claim: Interoceptive perturbation via CCRC (controlled CO₂ rebreathing challenge: 5% CO₂/95% O₂, 90-second exposure) increases PCI in MCS patients but not VS/UWS; perturbation-evoked ΔPCI exceeds arousal-matched white-noise control.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: PCI increase ≥ 10% in MCS post-CCRC (paired t p < 0.05); VS/UWS group: no significant change; PCI change correlates with HEP change (r > 0.4); group × stimulation interaction significant (p < 0.05); CCRC-evoked ΔPCI exceeds white-noise control ΔPCI (paired t p < 0.05).
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: No significant PCI change post-CCRC in MCS (ΔPCI < 5%, p > 0.10); or equivalent PCI change in VS/UWS and MCS; or CCRC-evoked PCI change statistically indistinguishable from white-noise control (p > 0.10), indicating arousal rather than interoceptive prediction error.

ccrc_protocol.mechanism: Brainstem chemoreceptor activation (carotid body, medullary chemosensors) producing visceral interoceptive prediction error without nociception
stopping_criteria: HR increase > 40 bpm from resting baseline
ccrc_protocol.contraindicated_patients: Receive white-noise condition only; analyzed in pre-specified sensitivity analysis
ccrc_protocol.cpt_exclusion: Cold Pressor Test explicitly excluded from DoC patient cohort on ethical grounds (nociceptive stimulus in population unable to provide meaningful voluntary withdrawal)

arousal_control.purpose: Dissociate interoceptive prediction error from general sympathetic arousal
arousal_control.comparison: CCRC ΔPCI must exceed white-noise ΔPCI (paired t p < 0.05)

confirming_thresholds.vsuws_no_change: VS/UWS shows no significant PCI change

falsifying_thresholds.condition_2: Equivalent PCI change in VS/UWS and MCS (no group × stimulation interaction, η² < 0.06)
falsifying_thresholds.condition_3: CCRC-evoked ΔPCI statistically indistinguishable from white-noise control (p > 0.10)

sub_predictions.claim: HEP amplitude correlates with GCS-S score at both 3-month and 6-month follow-up (Spearman r > 0.4 within-participant), tracking longitudinal recovery trajectory. Joint HEP + PCI baseline model predicts 6-month recovery status with PPV ≥ 70% and NPV ≥ 70%.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: Within-participant Spearman r(HEP, GCS-S) > 0.4 at both 3-month and 6-month follow-ups; joint baseline model PPV ≥ 70% and NPV ≥ 70% for 6-month recovery status.
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: HEP shows no significant longitudinal correlation with GCS-S (r < 0.2) at either follow-up; or joint model predictive values do not exceed clinical benchmarks.

missing_data_protocol.locf_exclusion: LOCF is EXPLICITLY EXCLUDED from all longitudinal analyses — LOCF assumes a static post-dropout state that is clinically false in DoC populations and introduces directional bias in predictive value estimates
missing_data_protocol.primary_model: JMbayes2 (Rizopoulos 2012) joint modelling of longitudinal GCS-S outcomes and time-to-event data (death or clinical withdrawal), treating informative dropout as structural
missing_data_protocol.sensitivity_analysis_mnar: Pattern-mixture models with δ-adjustment (δ = 0, −0.5 SD, −1.0 SD) for MNAR robustness
missing_data_protocol.robustness_criterion: Pred 7.D classified as robust only if joint model and MICE sensitivity estimates agree in sign and both meet ΔR² ≥ 0.05 at p < 0.05

confirming_thresholds.followup_6mo_status: PRIMARY CONFIRMATORY (both required per DOCX registry update 2026-06-19)

falsifying_thresholds.condition: No significant longitudinal correlation with GCS-S at either follow-up

sub_predictions.claim: Somatic bias (β_SM) modulates the reportable content weighting of the global workspace, shifting subjective self-report profiles toward 'bodily/visceral' dimensions under high-β attentional focus (Tier 3 access proxy; L4 phenomenal claims excluded).
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: Body focus condition yields elevated 'bodily/visceral' subscale ratings (d > 0.45) and reduced 'detached/cognitive' ratings; effect survives arousal covariation (pupil diameter, HRV).
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: Manipulation produces no effect on reportable embodiment dimensions (d < 0.20); or effect is fully absorbed by general arousal covariates.

instruments: Phenomenology of Consciousness Inventory (PCI; Pekala, 1991) — state-adapted subscales, treated strictly as Tier 3 behavioral proxies
Multidimensional Assessment of Interoceptive Awareness (MAIA; Mehling et al., 2012)
operationalization.manipulation: Body focus (high-β) attentional condition vs. exteroceptive focus control
operationalization.tier_note: Tier 3 access proxy only — does NOT constitute direct measure of phenomenal qualia (L4 excluded per APGI framework)

confirming_thresholds.target_dimension: bodily/visceral subscale elevation AND detached/cognitive reduction
confirming_thresholds.covariate_survival: Effect must survive covariation for pupil diameter and HRV

falsifying_thresholds.condition_2: Effect fully absorbed by general arousal covariates
falsifying_thresholds.scope_note: This scoped falsifier does not bear on the core hierarchical ignition account (Pred 7.A–7.D)

sub_predictions.exploratory_note: Exploratory prediction. Confirmation not required for primary DoC-Biomarker claims (Pred 7.A–7.D). Tests the downstream behavioral readout of the β_SM → Πⁱ_eff → θₜ pathway at the Tier 3 access level.

validation_notes: Formally specified. Directly supported by Paper 3 Appendix (Disorders of Consciousness Table). Updated 2026-06-19 per DOCX registry (EP-7, Paper 3): n_target increased to 110 (VS/UWS=30, MCS=30, EMCS=20, controls=30); Pred 7.C updated to CCRC protocol (CO₂ rebreathing replacing heartbeat attention task); Pred 7.D updated with JMbayes2 joint modelling and explicit LOCF exclusion; Pred 7.E reframed from somatic-bias-PQS to embodiment-weighting exploratory; 6-month follow-up now primary confirmatory (both 3-mo and 6-mo) per DOCX.

participants.power_basis: Mixed-effects ordinal logistic regression across four DoC groups (equal allocation) requires N = 24 per group (total N = 96) to detect medium effect (OR = 2.5, α = 0.05, power = 0.80; Whitehead 1993 proportional odds formula). N = 110 target incorporates 15% attrition.
participants.emcs_note: EMCS group (N=20) required for four-group gradient confirmation (Pred 7.B). If insufficient EMCS patients recruited (n < 10), Pred 7.B four-group gradient downgraded to exploratory; three-group (VS/UWS, MCS, controls) treated as primary.
participants.blinding_note: Administering neurologist strictly blinded to PCI and HEP biomarker results to prevent diagnostic expectation bias.
inclusion_criteria: Clinical DoC diagnosis by certified neurologist using CRS-R and GCS-S
Stable medical condition permitting EEG and TMS assessment
Etiology: traumatic or hypoxic-ischemic brain injury
exclusion_criteria: Active seizure disorder or anti-epileptic drug modification within 2 weeks
Significant cardiac arrhythmia precluding reliable ECG R-peak detection
Metallic implants precluding TMS (for PCI sub-study)
healthy_controls_criteria: No history of neurological or psychiatric disorder
Age- and sex-matched to patient groups

apparatus.EEG: 64-channel BrainAmp DC (Brain Products GmbH) at 1000 Hz, bedside-compatible cap
apparatus.ECG: BrainAmp ExG bipolar lead-II synchronised to EEG
apparatus.TMS_for_PCI: Magstim Rapid² with 70 mm figure-of-eight coil; vertex stimulation protocol for PCI
apparatus.interoceptive_stimulation: CCRC (Controlled CO₂ Rebreathing Challenge): 5% CO₂/95% O₂ via non-rebreather mask, 90-second exposure; continuous SpO₂ and end-tidal CO₂ monitoring; stopping criteria: SpO₂ drop > 5%, ETCO₂ > 60 mmHg, or HR increase > 40 bpm
apparatus.arousal_matched_control: Auditory white noise, 70 dB, 90 seconds; administered in counterbalanced order with CCRC to dissociate interoceptive prediction error from general sympathetic arousal
apparatus.dmn_connectivity: Resting-state EEG source analysis (sLORETA or beamforming) for DMN proxy; or resting fMRI if clinically feasible (Pred 7.B DMN criteria)

target.type: TMS pulse for PCI; auditory heartbeat for interoceptive stimulation condition; auditory noise for arousal-matched control condition

trial_structure.response: Passive EEG recording; no behavioural response required from patients. Controls perform auditory detection task.

sessions.baseline: Enrolment (week 0): EEG resting state + PCI + CRS-R + GCS-S + DMN proxy
sessions.stimulation: Week 1: CCRC block + post-CCRC PCI + white-noise control block + post-control PCI (counterbalanced order)
sessions.followup_3mo: Month 3: EEG + PCI + CRS-R + GCS-S (PRIMARY CONFIRMATORY for Pred 7.D)
sessions.followup_6mo: Month 6: EEG + PCI + CRS-R + GCS-S (PRIMARY CONFIRMATORY for Pred 7.D — both 3-mo and 6-mo required per DOCX 2026-06-19)

eeg_analysis_pipeline.ica_cardiac_artifact: Automated ICA cardiac component rejection (EEGLAB ADJUST)
eeg_analysis_pipeline.pci_computation_software: Comolatti et al. (2019) open-source toolbox; PCI threshold for consciousness = 0.31
eeg_analysis_pipeline.dmn_analysis: Resting-state connectivity: ICA-derived DMN component or seed-based connectivity (PCC seed); correlation with HEP amplitude and PCI per participant

dependent_variables: HEP amplitude (250–400 ms, Cz) per DoC group and time point — primary (Pred 7.A, 5.b, 5.d)
PCI per DoC group and time point — primary (Pred 7.A, 5.b)
ΔR² (joint HEP + PCI model vs. best univariate model) for 3-month GCS-S — PRIMARY Pred 7.A
AUC (joint model) for binary recovery outcome — aspirational secondary Pred 7.A only
Univariate model R² for HEP alone and PCI alone (comparison benchmarks for Pred 7.A)
Four-group ordinal gradient: HEP and PCI across VS/UWS, MCS, EMCS, controls — Pred 7.B
DMN–HEP partial correlation (r, controlling for PCI) — Pred 7.B
ΔPCI post-CCRC: MCS group (% change from baseline) — Pred 7.C
ΔPCI post-white-noise control: MCS group (% change) — Pred 7.C arousal comparison
Group × stimulation interaction on ΔPCI — Pred 7.C
Spearman r(HEP amplitude, GCS-S) at 3-month follow-up within-participant — confirmatory Pred 7.D
Spearman r(HEP amplitude, GCS-S) at 6-month follow-up within-participant — confirmatory Pred 7.D
JMbayes2 joint model: longitudinal GCS-S + time-to-event — Pred 7.D
PPV and NPV: joint model prediction of 6-month recovery status — Pred 7.D
primary_hypothesis: A linear model including both HEP amplitude and PCI at enrolment explains ΔR² ≥ 0.05 more variance in 3-month GCS-S outcome than either biomarker alone. HEP amplitude and PCI show a four-group gradient (VS/UWS < MCS < EMCS < controls), interoceptive perturbation increases PCI ≥ 10% in MCS but not VS/UWS with ΔPCI exceeding arousal-matched control by ≥ 5%, and HEP amplitude correlates with 3-month GCS-S (Spearman r > 0.4).
primary_analysis: Pred 7.A: Multiple linear regression GCS-S_3mo ~ HEP + PCI + age + etiology + (random site effect); LRT vs. univariate models; ΔR² as primary metric. Ordinal logistic regression for categorical consciousness level (VS/UWS / MCS / EMCS / recovered). Pred 7.B: Mann–Whitney U (MCS vs. VS/UWS); Kruskal-Wallis + Jonckheere–Terpstra across four groups; DMN–PCI and partial DMN–HEP correlations. Pred 7.C: paired t-tests for ΔPCI (CCRC vs. baseline; CCRC vs. white-noise control); Pearson r for PCI–HEP change; group × stimulation interaction. Pred 7.D: JMbayes2 joint model of longitudinal GCS-S and time-to-event; MICE (m=50) as sensitivity analysis; pattern-mixture models for MNAR robustness; LOCF explicitly excluded; PPV/NPV from binary recovery classification. Etiology (traumatic vs. hypoxic-ischemic) included as covariate in all models.

power_analysis.software: G*Power 3.1; Whitehead (1993) proportional odds formula for ordinal logistic regression
power_analysis.based_on: Rosanova et al. (2018) PCI in DoC (d = 0.6 for VS/UWS vs. controls); Canales-Johnson et al. (2020) HEP group differences; mixed-effects ordinal logistic regression OR = 2.5 across 4 groups

pre_registration.locf_exclusion: LOCF is explicitly excluded from all longitudinal analyses (Pred 7.D). JMbayes2 joint model is primary; MICE (m=50) is pre-specified sensitivity analysis.
pre_registration.amendment_note: 6-month follow-up is now PRIMARY CONFIRMATORY (not exploratory) per DOCX registry update 2026-06-19; no OSF amendment required for 6-month analysis.

ethics.committee: Institutional Review Board and Research Ethics Committee (clinical)
ethics.surrogate_consent: Required for all patient participants; surrogate (next-of-kin or legal guardian) provides written informed consent

caveats: PCI requires TMS, which cannot be applied to patients with metallic implants or unstable hemodynamics; PCI subsample may be smaller than full cohort.

HEP reliability in DoC populations is lower than in healthy controls due to cardiac artifact overlap; minimum 20 clean epochs per participant required; participants below this threshold excluded from HEP analyses.
Etiology (traumatic vs. hypoxic-ischemic) is a known moderator of recovery trajectory and is included as covariate in all models.
EMCS group (Pred 7.B four-group gradient) requires N = 20; if n < 10 EMCS, four-group confirmatory test is downgraded to exploratory and three-group (VS/UWS, MCS, controls) is primary.

Pred 7.C CCRC is contraindicated in patients with severe COPD, active seizure disorder, or hemodynamic instability; these patients receive white-noise condition only and are analyzed in pre-specified sensitivity analysis. Cold Pressor Test is explicitly excluded from DoC patient cohort.
Pred 7.D uses JMbayes2 joint modelling; LOCF is explicitly excluded. The 6-month follow-up is now PRIMARY CONFIRMATORY (not exploratory) per DOCX registry update 2026-06-19.

An independent DSMB oversees all patient recruitment and CCRC sessions. CCRC stopping criteria (SpO₂ drop > 5%, ETCO₂ > 60 mmHg, HR > 40 bpm above baseline) are absolute; a dedicated nursing observer monitors all patient CCRC sessions continuously.
PCI subsample may be smaller than full HEP cohort due to TMS contraindications; all analyses distinguish the full HEP cohort (N up to 110) from the PCI subsample.
